2606.26917.title: GEOALIGN: Geometric Rollout Curation for Robust LLM Reinforcement Learning
2606.26917.paper_summary: Online reinforcement learning is widely used to align large language models (LLMs) with reward signals, yet training can be unstable under noisy or misspecified rewards. We identify a failure mode we call directional inconsistency: within a batch, a small set of high-reward rollouts induces representation-space preference directions that sharply disagree with the batch majority, resulting in high-variance and destabilizing updates. We propose geoalign, a lightweight plug-in for rollout curation in iterative policy optimization. Geoalign (i) forms within-prompt preference pairs, (ii) learns an online projector on per-rollout hidden states to concentrate reward-ordered displacement directions, and (iii) detects directionally inconsistent rollouts via their angular deviation from a batch consensus prototype and rectifies them with within-prompt stable alternatives. Geoalign is forward-pass only and adds negligible overhead. Across dialogue alignment with a learned reward model and mathematical reasoning with binary verified rewards, Geoalign improves final performance and reduces training oscillation, outperforming PF-PPO, PAR, PODS, and Seed-GRPO. These results suggest latent directional consensus as an effective reliability signal for online LLM RL.
2606.26917.paper_summary_zh: 線上強化學習被廣泛用於將大型語言模型（LLMs）與獎勵信號對齊，然而在噪聲或錯誤指定的獎勵下，訓練可能會不穩定。我們識別出一種我們稱之為方向不一致的失敗模式：在一個批次內，一小組高獎勵的回合導致的表示空間偏好方向與批次的大多數 sharply disagree，從而導致高方差和不穩定的更新。我們提出了geoalign，一個輕量級的插件，用於迭代策略優化中的回合策劃。Geoalign (i) 在提示內形成偏好對，(ii) 在每個回合的隱藏狀態上學習一個在線投影器，以集中獎勵排序的位移方向，並 (iii) 通過它們與批次共識原型的角度偏差檢測方向不一致的回合，並用提示內的穩定替代方案進行修正。Geoalign僅需前向傳遞，並增加微不足道的開銷。在使用學習獎勵模型的對話對齊和使用二元驗證獎勵的數學推理中，Geoalign改善了最終性能並減少了訓練振盪，超越了PF-PPO、PAR、PODS和Seed-GRPO。這些結果表明潛在的方向共識是在線LLM RL的一個有效可靠性信號。
2606.26917.author: Ting Zhou et.al.
2606.26917.authors: Ting Zhou, Zhenqing Ling, Yiyang Zhao, Ying Shen, Daoyuan Chen
